s3¶
Description¶
This section explains prominent concepts and notations in the set of high-level S3 commands provided.
If you are looking for the low level S3 commands for the CLI, please see the s3api command reference page.
Path Argument Type¶
Whenever using a command, at least one path argument must be specified. There are two types of path arguments: LocalPath and S3Uri.
LocalPath: represents the path of a local file or directory. It can be written as an absolute path or relative path.
Order of Path Arguments¶
Single Local File and S3 Object Operations¶
Some commands perform operations only on single files and S3 objects. The following commands are single file/object operations if no --recursive flag is provided.

For this type of operation, the first path argument, the source, must exist and be a local file or S3 object. The second path argument, the destination, can be the name of a local file, local directory, S3 object, S3 prefix, or S3 bucket.
Directory and S3 Prefix Operations¶
Use of Exclude and Include Filters¶
• *: Matches everything
• ?: Matches any single character
• [sequence]: Matches any character in sequence
• [!sequence]: Matches any character not in sequence
All files will be excluded from the command except for files ending with .txt However, if the order of the filter parameters was changed to
All files will be excluded from the command.

Each filter is evaluated against the source directory. If the source location is a file instead of a directory, the directory containing the file is used as the source directory. For example, suppose you had the following directory structure:
If you wanted to include both .jpg files as well as .txt files you can run:

agent_content_hints.rationale: PRD F2.2/F2.3: retrieval-lift evidence deferred to Phase 6

caveats: Single corpus, single grader architecture, single run.
Four pillars carry zero headline weight in v2; still reported as diagnostics.
Profile-specific weights collapse to the same 2-pillar composite pending corpus-003.

Cross-judge accuracy variance: per-judge corpus-002 means span 0.591-0.698 across Llama-3.3-70B, GPT-4o, and DeepSeek-V3.2 (pooled kappa 0.706-0.817). The v2 composite-vs-accuracy Pearson r ranges from +0.440 to +0.618 across judges (all above the +0.35 ship gate). Report majority-vote CI [0.567, 0.688] or per-judge union CI [0.530, 0.758] when comparing across studies.
